---
title: "nitrix.stats.lme"
generated: true
signature-hash: 300758ae20bd7572
description: "Voxelwise linear mixed-effects models."
listing:
  id: nitrix-stats-lme-symbols
  contents: "nitrix-stats-lme/*.qmd"
  type: table
  fields: [title, description]
  sort: "title"
  filter-ui: false
  page-size: 500
---

::: {.callout-important title="Generated reference stub — fill me"}
Auto-generated scaffold for `nitrix.stats.lme`. Fill the prose *outside* the
generated markers; do **not** ship as-is (SPEC §6.5). `check_reference_staleness.py` flags signature drift.
:::

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:__module__ -->
Voxelwise linear mixed-effects models.

Two layered primitives are exposed:

- [`reml_fit`](nitrix-stats-lme-reml/reml_fit.qmd) -- general variance-components REML via the
  FaST-LMM spectral trick.  For a model
  $V = \sigma_b^2 ZZ^{\top} + \sigma_e^2 I$, the term
  $ZZ^{\top}$ is eigendecomposed once (shared across
  voxels); `y` and `X` are rotated into the diagonalising
  basis, so the per-voxel inner loop becomes $O(N)$ per
  Newton step instead of the naive $O(N^3)$.  The fit is
  vmap-batched over voxels with a shared design.
- [`flame_two_level`](nitrix-stats-lme-flame/flame_two_level.qmd) -- the FSL FLAME equivalent: the
  per-subject within-variance is known (from a level-1 GLM) and
  the between-subject variance is estimated.  Here $V$ is
  naturally diagonal, so the spectral trick is trivial.  This is
  built on top of the general REML inner solver.

Both fits use Newton scoring on the log-parameterised variance
components, which makes positivity automatic and gives quadratic
local convergence.  A fixed-iteration scan (default 20) is used
for clean vmap composition; the caller can tune this via the
`n_iter` argument.  The output statistics are differentiable
via an implicit-function-theorem VJP.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen sections:__module__ -->
### References

.. [1] Lippert, C., Listgarten, J., Liu, Y., Kadie, C. M.,
   Davidson, R. I., & Heckerman, D. (2011). FaST linear mixed
   models for genome-wide association studies. Nature Methods,
   8(10), 833-835. https://doi.org/10.1038/nmeth.1681
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen dims:__page__ -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `V` | elements of a mass-univariate map — voxels, vertices, or fixels |
| `p` | fixed-effect coefficients / design-matrix columns |
| `n_v` | *(undocumented — add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
| `r` | *(undocumented — add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
| `N` | observations — time points, subjects, or sampled points |
| `q` | random-effect columns |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## Symbols

::: {#nitrix-stats-lme-symbols}
:::
